current_fact.fact_text: On April 25, 1945, delegates from around the world gathered in San Francisco for the United Nations Conference on International Organization. In the shadow of a global war, they began designing a new institution meant to keep future conflicts from reaching the same catastrophic scale.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

current_fact.match_reason: The birth of the UN is a reflective transition from war to fragile cooperation, resonating with an episode about healing, looking back, and slowly building a different future.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1792, Claude Joseph Rouget de Lisle composed “La Marseillaise,” a song written in a single revolutionary night that would later become France’s national anthem. What began as a marching tune for soldiers of the Rhine became one of the world’s most recognizable musical calls to freedom.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

queue.match_reason: The creation of a national anthem that becomes a defining soundtrack mirrors Sedna FM's very first episode, which serves as the 'anthem' and origin point for the whole station.

queue.fact_text: On April 25, 1898, the U.S. Congress retroactively declared that a state of war with Spain had existed since April 21, following a naval blockade of Cuba. The brief Spanish–American War would end with Spain losing most of its remaining colonies, reshaping maps from the Caribbean to the Pacific.

episode.title: Morning Drops #38 - History of Flamenco
episode.description: A special journey into the history of Flamenco, guided by Yasmin and her guest Almudena from Madrid, Spain. Exploring the roots and evolution of this emotionally raw and culturally rich tradition, from its early vocal origins and Andalusian influences, through its transformation on stage and screen, to its modern reinventions by iconic Spanish artists.
songs: La Niña de los Peines - Al Gurugú
Paco de Lucía - Entre dos aguas
Camarón de la Isla - La leyenda del tiempo

queue.match_reason: A turning point in Spain’s imperial history sits alongside an episode dedicated to Spain’s deep musical soul, spotlighting cultural richness beyond its wars.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1882, French officer Henri Rivière seized the citadel of Hanoi with a relatively small force of marine infantry, provoking clashes in Tonkin. This audacious move helped set the stage for full French colonial control over northern Vietnam.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance. Music shaped by sand, memory, and survival.

queue.match_reason: A colonial incursion in Vietnam echoes with an episode about Tuareg music and the sound of peoples navigating colonization, exile, and resistance across another continent.

queue.fact_text: On April 25, 1829, Captain Charles Fremantle arrived off the coast of what is now Western Australia in HMS Challenger, preparing to claim the Swan River Colony for the British Empire. His arrival marked the beginning of a new colonial outpost on lands long inhabited by Aboriginal peoples.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

queue.match_reason: A Royal Navy vessel arriving to claim distant shores aligns with a sea-themed sailing episode, offering a softer, modern counterpoint to historic voyages of empire.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1959, the Saint Lawrence Seaway officially opened, linking the Great Lakes to the Atlantic Ocean. It turned the heart of North America into a deepwater highway, allowing ocean-going ships to reach cities thousands of kilometers inland.

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

queue.match_reason: A newly opened waterway connecting continents fits with a boat-themed musical journey across seas and coasts, both celebrating water as a corridor between cultures.

queue.fact_text: On April 25, 1980, Dan-Air Flight 1008 crashed into a mountain near Tenerife after a navigation error, killing all 146 people on board. The tragedy led to changes in air-traffic procedures and highlighted how a chain of small mistakes can become catastrophic in the air.

episode.title: Morning Drops #35 - Thailand
episode.description: Recorded in Koh Samui, Thailand, where Yasmin and her friend Patricia spent more than two intense weeks training Muay Thai and Western boxing. Between sweat, rhythm, and sea breeze, they share reflections on discipline, friendship, and the beauty of movement - with a soundtrack that connects Thai sounds to the flow of daily practice.
songs: Burna Boy - Cheat on Me (feat. Dave)

queue.match_reason: A serious aviation disaster sits beside an episode about resilience, discipline, and physical training, offering an emotional counterweight focused on care for the body and daily practice.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1981, over 100 workers at Japan’s Tsuruga Nuclear Power Plant were exposed to radiation during maintenance work. The incident, caused by poor procedures and leaks, became one of Japan’s early warnings about the risks of nuclear power management.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

queue.match_reason: A nuclear-plant radiation accident involving electricity and danger is paired, in a darkly thematic way, with an 'Electric Shock' episode that channels raw electric energy through music instead of reactors.

queue.fact_text: On April 25, 1982, Israel completed its withdrawal from the Sinai Peninsula in accordance with the Camp David Accords. The handover, which included dismantling settlements, was a rare example of a negotiated land-for-peace agreement reshaping a tense border.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

queue.match_reason: A hard-won peace involving desert borders finds a gentle echo in an episode about mountains, landscapes, and reflective journeys through shared stories and songs.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1983, 10-year-old American schoolgirl Samantha Smith received an invitation from Soviet leader Yuri Andropov after she wrote to him about her fear of nuclear war. Her subsequent visit to the USSR turned her into a child ambassador of peace at the height of the Cold War.

episode.title: Sedna FM #03 - Beautiful as You Feel
episode.description: An episode about self-acceptance and inner beauty. Music that reminds us we are beautiful as we feel, embracing our authentic selves. Warm, uplifting sounds that celebrate individuality.
songs: The The - this is the day
DCTL - Deep Tannin f(eat Rafael Leafar)

queue.match_reason: A young girl bravely writing to a superpower about peace reflects themes of innocence, inner courage, and belief in one’s own voice that align with an episode about inner beauty and authenticity.

queue.fact_text: On April 25, 2005, the final piece of the ancient Obelisk of Axum was returned from Italy to Ethiopia, decades after it had been taken by Mussolini’s regime. The 1,700-year-old monument’s homecoming was celebrated as a symbolic act of cultural restitution and healing.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

queue.match_reason: The return of a stolen African monument pairs naturally with an episode celebrating African musical heritage and the global influence of Afrobeat.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 2014, officials in Flint, Michigan switched the city’s water supply to the Flint River, setting off a crisis of lead and bacterial contamination. Residents’ brown tap water and health complaints were dismissed for months, turning a cost-cutting decision into a public-health catastrophe.

episode.title: Morning Drops #54 - Intentional Listening
episode.description:
Do you remember what it felt like to listen to music without a streaming platform?
In this Morning Drops episode, Gaia reflects on a recent experiment: stepping away from algorithmic listening and going back to an iPod. 

What starts as a small personal choice opens into a wider meditation on attention, presence, and what happens when music is no longer treated as something infinite, but as something we choose with care.

Tracklist:
Scientist - Drum Song Dub
The Other People Place - Let Me Be Me
A Forest Mighty Black - Fresh In My Mind
Cymande - Bra
songs: The Other People Place - Let Me Be Me
A Forest Mighty Black - Fresh In My Mind

queue.match_reason: A crisis born partly from ignoring citizens' voices is matched with an episode about listening with intention, underlining the importance of paying real attention to what flows around us—whether water or information.

queue.fact_text: On April 25, 2015, a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck Nepal, killing nearly 9,000 people and devastating historic sites and mountain villages. The tremor shifted the landscape itself, triggering avalanches on Everest and exposing the vulnerability of communities living amid the Himalayas.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

queue.match_reason: A powerful reminder of Earth's raw tectonic force is balanced by an episode immersed in another extreme landscape—the Arctic ocean—both highlighting the planet’s beauty and fragility.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1960s-style technological fashion, but in 2015's world, the cumulative effect of the Flint and Nepal events showed how fragile human structures are compared to the Earth’s forces—and how quickly communities mobilize to help. Relief efforts, citizen science, and grassroots activism turned tragedy into a catalyst for new resilience.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: This meta-reflection on recent disasters and global response connects with an episode about travel, shifting perspectives, and quietly rethinking how we move through the world.

queue.fact_text: On April 25, 1960s-era tensions looked very different at sea, underground, and in orbit—but all shared one thread: humans pushing into hostile environments they could barely survive without technology. From subs circumnavigating the globe to spacecraft heading for the outer planets, engineering became our way of stretching where 'home' could be.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

queue.match_reason: This broader reflection on exploration matches an episode explicitly framed as a voyage into new territories, both turning movement and curiosity into a journey metaphor.

queue.fact_text: On this day, water, stone, and sound all share a hidden thread: from seaways being opened and rivers poisoned, to ancient obelisks returned and national anthems born. Each reminds us that landscapes and cultures carry long memories, and that what humans build can either scar or heal the places we inhabit.

episode.title: Morning Drops #18 - Sardegna
episode.description: Yasmin traces her connection to Sardegna — the island where her Italian journey began. Through music and memory, she shares the story of how this place shaped her family's path, and why it still holds such a deep place in her heart.
songs: Mahmood & Tenores di Bitti - Com’è profondo il mare

queue.match_reason: A reflection on heritage, land, and long memories resonates with an episode rooted in a specific place—Sardinia—and its emotional and cultural landscape.

queue.fact_text: Across all these events that fell on April 25—wars declared and ended, atoms split and sequenced, rivers opened and contaminated, children writing to presidents and scientists decoding life—one pattern emerges: a single date can hold both destruction and astonishing creativity. History is less a straight line and more a mixtape of human choices, remixed every year.

episode.title: Evening Flows #00 - Introduction to evening flows
episode.description:
Sedna FM introduces a new format: Evening Flow, where you can enjoy curated music sets from selectors, DJs, and artists.

Each mix is an invitation to drift beyond the expected. A space where hidden gems from personal archives come to light, where sound speaks its own language. There are no rules here, no patterns to follow. Just the steady unfolding of a mood, a texture, a journey shaped by feeling.

Let the music lead the way - enjoy the trip!

queue.match_reason: This closing meta-fact about April 25 as a whole aligns with the open-ended, format-introducing Evening Flow episode, both inviting listeners to see patterns and journeys across many different pieces.

published.fact_text: On April 25, 1983, the spacecraft Pioneer 10 quietly slipped beyond the orbit of Pluto, becoming the first human-made object to travel so far from the Sun. Launched in 1972, it had already flown past Jupiter and then just kept going—its faint radio signals turning into a long-distance heartbeat from the edge of the Solar System.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A pioneering spacecraft moving into deep space pairs with an episode about reimagining 'emptiness' as creative space and shifting perspective.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1953, Francis Crick and James Watson published their paper describing the double-helix structure of DNA. In just a few pages, they revealed the elegant spiral code that underlies heredity, turning the molecule of life into a readable library of biological information.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

published.match_reason: The DNA discovery is a landmark in scientific understanding and connects well with an episode about learning, curiosity, and how new knowledge reshapes us.

published.fact_text: On April 25, 1954, Bell Telephone Laboratories publicly demonstrated the first practical solar cell. Their tiny silicon device could turn sunlight directly into electricity, hinting at a future where rooftops and deserts could become quiet power plants.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

published.match_reason: Solar cells and the idea of harvesting sunlight tie into a calm, eco-flavoured "Slow Planet" mood that imagines gentler, more sustainable rhythms on Earth.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1961, Robert Noyce was granted a patent for the monolithic integrated circuit. By etching multiple electronic components onto a single chip of silicon, he helped shrink rooms full of hardware into something that could eventually fit in a pocket.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

published.match_reason: The integrated circuit made modern phones and computers possible, which fits perfectly with an episode reflecting on our deep and complicated relationship with those devices.

published.fact_text: On April 25, 1960, the U.S. Navy’s nuclear submarine USS Triton completed the first fully submerged circumnavigation of Earth. For 60 days it slipped silently under the waves, proving that a vessel could sail around the world without ever surfacing.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

published.match_reason: A secretive submarine voyage beneath the sea mirrors the episode's theme of immersion and going below the surface into fluid, water-like states.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1945, American and Soviet troops met at the Elbe River near Torgau, effectively cutting Nazi Germany in two. The handshake between soldiers who had been strangers in both language and ideology became a powerful symbol of unity in the final days of World War II.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

published.match_reason: Elbe Day marks the beginning of the end of a dark war, aligning with an episode about weathering storms and moving into a more hopeful, post-crisis chapter.

published.fact_text: Also on April 25, 1945, Italy’s National Liberation Committee called for a general uprising against Nazi occupation and the Italian Social Republic, an event now commemorated as Liberation Day. It marked an eruption of partisan courage that helped push fascism out of northern Italy.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

published.match_reason: An anti-fascist uprising and a national liberation holiday fit naturally with an episode about politically charged music and musical protest against systems of power.

published.fact_text: On April 25, 1945, delegates from around the world gathered in San Francisco for the United Nations Conference on International Organization. In the shadow of a global war, they began designing a new institution meant to keep future conflicts from reaching the same catastrophic scale.
